mapping.a_element: Legendre-invariant geometrothermodynamic formalism for black hole thermodynamics
mapping.b_element: Ruppeiner geometry applied to black hole thermodynamics

mapping.a_element: curvature of the space of equilibrium states indicating thermodynamic interaction and phase transitions
mapping.b_element: non-flat Ruppeiner state space and diverging curvature at extremal limits

mapping.a_element: Weinhold's and Ruppeiner's thermodynamic metrics for black holes
mapping.b_element: Ruppeiner scalar and curvature as a measure of interactions

mapping.a_element: Reissner-Nordström black hole exhibiting curved geometry and phase transitions
mapping.b_element: Reissner-Nordström black hole with non-flat state space and diverging curvature

brief_justification: Both papers employ geometric frameworks (geometrothermodynamics and Ruppeiner geometry) to analyze black hole thermodynamics, where the curvature of the thermodynamic state space serves as an indicator of interactions and phase transitions. The abstracts explicitly link non-flat geometry to critical behavior, demonstrating shared underlying machinery.
